data.content: No conflicts here — each package appears in only one project. I'll create `Directory.Packages.props` and strip `Version` from all `.csproj` files.

arguments.file_text:
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>true</ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.AspNetCore.OpenApi" Version="8.0.24" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.12.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting" Version="1.15.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="System.Text.Json" Version="9.0.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.9.3" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="2.8.2" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>

data.reasoningText:
Now I have a clear picture. Let me create the `Directory.Packages.props` file and update all three `.csproj` files.

Packages across all projects:
- System.Text.Json 9.0.0 (Core)
- OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting 1.15.0 (Web)
- Microsoft.AspNetCore.OpenApi 8.0.24 (Web)
- Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk 17.12.0 (Tests)
- xunit 2.9.3 (Tests)
- xunit.runner.visualstudio 2.8.2 (Tests)


Since there are no version conflicts, I'll set up the centralized package management by creating the props file with all package versions defined, then strip out the version attributes from each project file while preserving other metadata.

arguments.file_text:
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>true</ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.AspNetCore.OpenApi" Version="8.0.24" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.12.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting" Version="1.15.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="System.Text.Json" Version="9.0.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.9.3" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="2.8.2" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>

data.content:
Done. Here's what I did:

- **Created `Directory.Packages.props`** at the solution root with `ManagePackageVersionsCentrally` enabled and all 6 package versions centralized (alphabetically sorted).
- **Stripped `Version` attributes** from `PackageReference` entries in all 3 `.csproj` files.
- **Preserved** other attributes like `PrivateAssets="all"` on `xunit.runner.visualstudio`.

No version conflicts existed — each package appeared in only one project.
